zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Lewis於1634年5月25日在英國肯特斯塔普勒爾斯特受洗時，他的父親George Lewis Jr.34歲，母親Mary Doggett33歲。他於1681年5月9日與Hannah Cobb在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1703年3月28日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波巴恩斯塔波享年68歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波巴恩斯塔波。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Lewis于1634年5月25日在英国肯特斯塔普勒爾斯特受洗时，他的父亲George Lewis Jr.34岁，母亲Mary Doggett33岁。他于1681年5月9日与Hannah Cobb在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1703年3月28日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波巴恩斯塔波享年68岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波巴恩斯塔波。

nameMeanings.name: Lewis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Lewis, Leweis, Lowis, from ancient Germanic (originally West Frankish) Hludwig (itself from hlōd- ‘fame, famous’ + wīg- ‘battle’). This was Latinized as Ludovicus and Chlodovisus, which were gallicized as Clovis or Clouis, French Louis. The name may also appear as Lawis, Laweys, Lawes, by unrounding of the vowel of Lowis on the analogy of the variation between Low and Law as pet forms of Middle English Lourence alias Laurence. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Laws 2 and compare Lawrence .
Welsh: adopted for the Welsh personal name Llywelyn (see Llewellyn ).

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh’. This is one of the most common Old Irish personal names. It is derived from Lugh ‘brightness’, which was the name of a Celtic god.

variants.name: Laws
meanings: English: variant of Law (pet form of Lawrence ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Perhaps a shortened form of Scottish or Irish   McLeish  . Compare   McLaws  .

variants.name: Lewison
meanings: Welsh and English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name   Lewis   (in Wales this was taken as equivalent to the Welsh personal name   Llewelyn  ). In North America, this surname has reportedly absorbed in some cases the Greek patronymic   Iliopoulos  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name Lewi, Polish-influenced form of Levy .

variants.name: McGlew
meanings: Irish: most probably from Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh’, a Gaelic personal name (referencing the god Lugh), sometimes Anglicized as Lewis . However, both Woulfe and MacLysaght consider McGlew to be a shortened form of Mac Dhuinnshléibhe (see McAleavey and Dunleavy ).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Lewis was christened on 25 May 1634, in Staplehurst, Kent, England, his father, George Lewis Jr., was 34 and his mother, Mary Doggett, was 33. He married Hannah Cobb on 9 May 1681, in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 28 March 1703, in Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 68, and was buried in Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Lewis è stato battezzato il 25 maggio 1634 nel Staplehurst, Kent, England, il suo padre, George Lewis Jr., aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Mary Doggett, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Hannah Cobb il 9 maggio 1681, nel Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 28 marzo 1703, nel Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Lewisが1634年5月25日にイギリスケントスタプレハルストで洗礼を受けた時，父George Lewis Jr.は34歳，母Mary Doggettは33歳であった。1681年5月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーバーンスタブルでHannah Cobbと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1703年3月28日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーバーンスタブルバーンスタブルで68歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーバーンスタブルバーンスタブルにて埋葬された。

ko.Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 플림우스

ko.Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay, British C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일

ko.Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일
ko.lifeSummary: Edward Lewis(이)가 1634년 5월 25일 영국 켄트 스타플레후르스트에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 George Lewis Jr.(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Doggett(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1681년 5월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일에서 Hannah Cobb(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1703년 3월 28일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 바른스타블일에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일 바른스타블일에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Lewis foi batizado em 25 de maio de 1634, em Staplehurst, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, George Lewis Jr., tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Mary Doggett, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Hannah Cobb em 9 de maio de 1681, em Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 28 de março de 1703, em Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Lewis был крещен 25 мая 1634 г. в местности стаплэурст, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени George Lewis Jr. было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Doggett, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Hannah Cobb 9 мая 1681 г. в местности барнстабл, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 28 марта 1703 г. в местности барнстабл, барнстабл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности барнстабл, барнстабл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Lewis se bautizó el 25 de mayo de 1634, en Staplehurst, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, George Lewis Jr., tenía 34 años y su mamá, Mary Doggett, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Hannah Cobb el 9 de mayo de 1681, en Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 28 de marzo de 1703, en Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Barnstable, Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
